observations.recommendation:
1.11 We reiterated our recommendation and the members of the Inventory
Committee agreed to:
1. Prioritize compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts to resolve the material discrepancies between PPE
balances in the books of account and in the physical count; and
1.12 We also reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Treasurer agreed
to:
2. Adopt property numbers using the numbering system prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2020-006 and provide property tags/stickers for each PPE; and
3. Conduct a complete physical count of all items categorized as PPE and
accordingly prepare the required inventory report.

2.21 We recommended and the GSO-Designate, in coordination with the concerned
requesting offices, agreed to conduct periodic physical inventory count of
supplies and materials and consequently submit the required RPCIs not later
than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester,
respectively.
2.22 We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate
agreed to maintain supplies ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, to record
the movement of all inventory items and as basis for the reconciliation of
inventories.
2.23 Moreover, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reclassify
the issued semi-expendable items to the corresponding semi-expendable
expense accounts, supported with the required documentation, using the
following proposed adjusting entries:
Account Dr. Cr.
Semi-expendable Machine and Equipment
1,576,288.00
Expense
Semi-expendable Furniture, Fixture and Books
266,340.00
Expense
Other Supplies and Materials Inventory 1,842,628.00
Reclassification of Semi-expendable items to the appropriate Expense Accounts
2.24 Further, we recommended and Management agreed to ensure proper
documentation for all inventory movements and initiate steps to comply and
implement the policies and procedures on the recording, monitoring and
issuance of inventories as embodied in the NGAS Manual for LGUs, especially
on the recognition of expense for supplies issued.

3.18 We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant,
agreed to:
1. Effect the following proposed adjusting entry to capitalize the interests
incurred as part of the cost of the qualifying asset; and
Water Supply Systems 6,525,295.77
Interest Expense 1,350,555.00
Prior Period Adjustments 5,174,740.77
To capitalize the borrowing costs directly attributable to the design and build
project of Sogod Water System (Binaliw Source) at Bagatayam, Sogod
2. Recompute the accumulated depreciation for fair presentation in the
financial statements.

4.11 We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant,
agreed to:
1. Reclassify the cash prizes awarded to the Municipality by effecting the
following proposed adjusting entry:
Due to LGUs 950,000.00
Miscellaneous Income 950,000.00
Reclassification of cash prizes received from various activities conducted by the
Province of Cebu
2. Perform a review or reconciliation of records with the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office to determine the cause of the negative balances and effect
the necessary adjustments accordingly; and
3. Make representation with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office to trace,
identify and settle the unaccounted shares totaling ₱2,751,192.23.

5.15 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exert efforts to
account the composition and specification of balances carried over from the
previous years, and take appropriate actions thereon, if these are already
accounted for.
5.16 We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to adjust the books
to cancel the reversion of outstanding checks, as follows:
Accounts Payable 9,955,986.89
Cash in Bank - LCC 9,955,986.89
To cancel the recorded adjustment/reversion of outstanding checks.

6.8 We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile their records and update or effect the
necessary adjustments, if any, to resolve variances in the Accounting and
Treasury records.

7.6 We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed
to comply with the requested documents by the GSIS for the reconciliation and
eventual remittance of the remaining premium contributions due.

8.10 We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Engineer, agreed
to:
1. Obtain revised/renewed performance and/or warranty surety bonds from the
contractor, with the appropriate validity period; and
2. Issue/Cause the issuance of Certificates of Final Acceptance only after the
defects liability period or one year from the completion date.

9.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor
agreed to reconcile their records pertaining to the parcels of land owned and
effect the necessary adjustments to present the correct Land account balance.
9.7 We also recommended and Management agreed to consider making
representation with the Registry of Deeds to request for copies of all contracts
of selling, transferring, or otherwise conveying, leasing, or mortgaging real
property received by, or acknowledged before their office to serve as a
reference/proof in the registration, transfer and titling of these parcels of land
and ensure ownership and full protection of the interest of the Municipality in
compliance with Section 39 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 92-386.

10.10 We recommended and the MDRRM Officer agreed to submit the
revised/supplemental LDRRMFIP and AIP for 2024 which includes the PPAs
charged to the unexpended balances of prior years’ LDRRMF to substantiate
the expenditures charged thereof pursuant to the provisions of NDRRMC-
DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

11.11 We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed
to submit the additional documents prescribed under COA Circular No. 2023-
008 in order to substantiate the request for write-off of the receivable accounts
totaling ₱237,712.00.

12.8 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the concerned
personnel to take action on the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the
occupants of the buildings owned and so that no fines, penalties, abatements or
closure orders are imposed on the Agency.

13.4 We recommended and Management, through the LDRRMO and Municipal
Accountant, agreed to submit to the Audit Team the Report on Sources and
Utilization of the LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each
month, and include in the report all information required in Annex B of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 to enable the Audit Team and other offices concerned to
timely conduct the evaluation of the LDRRMF utilization.

14.6 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to issue an Executive Order
creating a GAD M and E Team in compliance with the PCW-DILG-DBM-
NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the Property and Accounting Units shall undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records, and that the Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the Property Unit and the PPELCs maintained by the Accounting Unit are reconciled.
We likewise recommended the Municipal Treasurer, as acting Property Officer:
Ensure that the PCs are updated by posting all unrecorded acquisition/receipt, issue/transfer and disposal of PPE, if any; and
Prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for all PPEs found unserviceable, obsolete and/or no longer needed.
We reiterate our recommendation that the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the GSO and Municipal Accountant to ensure that the discrepancies between the RPCPPE, PPELCs, and PCs shall be investigated, verified, and adjusted. The total reconciled balances for each PPE account shall be established as the correct balance of PPE at year-end.
Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant update the PPELCs as necessary in the course of reconciliation and ensure that the total balance of PPELCs/SLs tally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledger.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the department heads' compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer together with the Municipal Assessor:
Trace and assess every component of the Road Networks such as road lots, road pavement, drainage and slope protection structures, and other miscellaneous structures and submit the report to the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer.
Assess the local road at every reporting date, whether there is any indication that a component of the local road may be impaired.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the local roads with identified cost as per report of the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Assessor.
Prepare and maintain Road Network Subsidiary Ledgers and separate subsidiary ledgers with complete description for each road and its components, such as road lots, road pavement, drainage and slope protection structures, and other miscellaneous structures and reconcile with the Local Road Inventory and Road Map to ensure completeness.
Prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Card, Annex D of COA Circular 2015-008.
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year over the prescribed useful life.
Provide disclosures on the total network system in the Notes to Financial Statements (Annex B of the Circular).
Finally, we recommended that the General Services Officer to:
Render a report on Local Road Network - Annex A of the Circular, at the end of the accounting period;
Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card tor all roads and its components, (Annex E) of the Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that transactions are recorded in the correct financial year in accordance with IPSAS 1 and PD 1445. Enhanced internal controls should be implemented to ensure that similar errors do not occur in the future, such as requiring a review process before finalizing entries for deposits.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant:
i. prepare the appropriate journal entry voucher to recognize the projects totaling P751,403.81 as Property, Plant and Equipment; and
ii. record the procured construction materials in the appropriate inventory account, and recognition of its issuance should be duly supported with Requisition and Issue Slip and Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI).
b. Municipal Treasurer as Property Custodian:
i. monitor and document the issuance of inventories to the end-user through the preparation of a Requisition and Issue Slip;
ii. summarize the issued Requisition and Issue Slip for the month in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued; and
iii. submit to the Municipal Accountant the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued, duly supported with the Requisition and Issue Slips, as basis for the recording of expense or construction-in-progress, as appropriate.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Accountant:
Reconcile the beginning balance recorded in the Subsidiary Ledger of Due to BIR account for the withheld taxes on government payments with the actual amount remitted to trace the amount of over-remittance and make the necessary adjustments. Henceforth, conduct regular reconciliation of the unremitted balances.
Strictly withhold taxes when income payment is paid or payable, or the income payment is accrued or recorded as an expense or asset, whichever is applicable, in the payor's books, whichever comes first and remit the taxes withheld within the period prescribed under the Revenue Regulations.
Require all professionals and contract-of-service workers to submit the appropriate sworn declarations before processing any payments.
Withhold taxes at the prescribed rates for the income payments made to the speakers and other professional fees regardless of the mode of disbursement/ payment.
Include all income payments made to employees for services rendered in addition to their regular salaries such as honoraria for BAC Members as part of their Gross Income and be subjected for withholding tax, if applicable; and
Municipal Treasurer Communicate with the Internet Provider to settle the unpaid balance by furnishing a copy of the Withholding Tax Certificates and ask for the issuance of Invoice for every payment made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor order the immediate remittance of the GSIS Contributions and Loan Amortizations based on the reconciled data of the Municipal Accountant and the Data Controller in-charge of the GSIS remittance in CY 2024.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
stop the practice of recording the loans under the account of “Other Payables” instead record the withholding of employees’ loan payment and other payables for remittance to the GSIS under Due to GSIS account.
trace back the loan balances of each employee based on the available records and determine the correct beginning balances to be carried over for CY 2025, and prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledger of each employee.
We likewise recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer:
reconcile records with GSIS and promptly update employees on any necessary changes to their employment status and salary adjustments in the GSIS Web MPS and prescribed GSIS format to ensure timely and accurate record updates.
monitor the approval of employees' loan applications and immediately inform the person-in-charge of the payroll and Municipal Accountant to ensure timely deductions from payroll.
We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, as the Office-in-charge of the payroll preparation, ensure that all premium contributions and loan amortizations are deducted from employees' salaries in the payroll for the month they are due.
Finally, we recommended that the Data Controller in-charge of GSIS remittances and the Municipal Accountant:
i. provide the Audit Team with an explanation of the discrepancies identified in the GSIS billing, the amounts withheld from contributions and loan deductions, and the corresponding remittances; and
ii. henceforth, conduct monthly reconciliations of employee premium contributions and loan amortizations by comparing GSIS billings with actual payroll deductions and remitted amounts. This practice ensures billing accuracy, facilitates timely remittances to GSIS, and guarantees correct salary deductions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
i. order the reconciliation on the Pag-IBIG Contributions and determine the correct amount of contributions to be remitted in CY 2024, and the immediate remittance of the employees and government share of Pag-IBIG Contributions, including the deficiencies; and
ii. assign the enrollment and update of members in the Pag-IBIG System to the Office of the Human Resource Management and not with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.
We also recommended the:
Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of recording the loans under the account of “Other Payables” instead record the withholding of employees’ premium payment and other payables for remittance to the Home Development Mutual Fund (HDMF) under Due to Pag-IBIG account.
Municipal Accountant to trace back the loan balances of each employee based on the available records and determine the correct beginning balances to be carried over for CY 2025, and prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledger of each employee.
c. Office of the Human Resource Management ensure that the records in the Pag-IBIG Fund are updated and the amount to be withheld in the payroll shall reconcile with the amount to be remitted as per the system of Pag-IBIG Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant:
The unremitted contributions in CY 2024 totaling P10,237.02 should be immediately remitted to the PhilHealth.
thoroughly account for the beginning balance of the "Due to PhilHealth" account amounting P163,633.02. Any necessary adjustments or corrections should be properly recorded, and all unremitted contributions must be promptly remitted.
Verify the deficiencies totaling P407,261.01 as noted in the Statement of Premium Account and make representation with the PhilHealth for the immediate settlement.
Additionally, we recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer reconcile records with PhilHealth and ensure timely updates of employees' employment status and salary adjustments in the Electronic Premium Remittance System (EPRS). The monthly PhilHealth contributions reflected in the EPRS should consistently match the amounts withheld and the LGU’s counterpart contribution.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a monthly reconciliation of PhilHealth contributions against the Preliminary Employees Premium Remittance List before processing the Disbursement Voucher for remittance.
Finally, we recommended for the refund by the person responsible the amount of penalty and surcharges incurred amounting to P5,009.74.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take proactive measures to ensure that all insurable LGU properties are covered under the General Insurance Fund (GIF) of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), in compliance with R.A. No. 656 and the guidelines outlined in COA Circular 2018-002 to mitigate the risk of losses resulting from calamities and unforeseen events.  Also:
i. Designate a person in-charge of the preparation of Property Inventory Form and the submission of the same to the Supervising Auditor/ Audit Team Leader and the GSIS not later than April 30 of each year; and
ii. Determine the latest appraised value of all insurable properties. An in-house appraisal shall be sufficient if the property or insurable interest has a value of P10 million and below. Otherwise, an independent appraisal shall be necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as Chairperson of the Municipal DRRM Council (MDRRMC) require prior review and evaluation of all PPAs before the Council approves them, and ensure that all funded projects align with eligible disaster preparedness, response, and recovery efforts as enumerated per thematic areas of DRRM under the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
Avoid granting the additional 25% overtime premium to Job Order workers in the absence of any contractual stipulation expressly providing for such benefit.
We further recommended that Municipal Budget Officer review the expense classification of obligations, and charge the employee compensation including overtime pay of the MDRRMO under Personnel Services and not with the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee Chairperson henceforth:
i. Invite a representative of the Commission on Audit, and at least two (2) observers, not only during bid evaluation but also during the eligibility checking, shortlisting, pre-bid conference, preliminary examination of bids, and post-qualification; and
ii Prepare and send the Request for Quotation to at least three (3) suppliers or contractors of known qualifications when using Small Value Procurement as an alternative method of procurement. This is notwithstanding those who responded through any of the required postings.
b. Municipal Health Officer process the DOH-licensing of Barangay Health Station of Barangay Pancho Villa as Birthing Facility, as well as its accreditation in the PhilHealth Maternity Care Package.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System-TWG to:
Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues.
Prepare a GAD Agenda identifying priority gender issues based on the GAD-related laws and policies, results of gender analysis and/or gender assessment, and results of program and project evaluation in terms of benefits to target beneficiaries.
Formulate GAD Plan and Budget based on the GAD Agenda and in accordance with the latest guidelines prescribed under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2024-01, dated December 2, 2024.
Enhance knowledge and skills in gender mainstreaming, gender analysis, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, and GAD-related tools through participation in relevant GAD training programs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor approve the Travel Orders of the Job Order personnel of the Municipality in the absence of proper delegation of such authority to another official.
We likewise recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
i. strictly observe compliance with the provisions of Executive Order No. 77 on the rates and apportionment of daily travel expenses; and
ii. refrain from processing disbursement vouchers on travel claims of Job Order personnel without an approved Travel Order, and the additional Certification from the Head of the Agency or any authorized approving official as required in Item 5 of the Specific Guidelines per COA Resolution No. 2021-044.

We recommended that the Local School Board furnish copies of the approved SEF Budget to the Local Budget Officer, the Local Accountant, and the Local Treasurer within seven (7) days from its approval. Furthermore, the quarterly and annual reports on SEF Utilization, prepared by the Municipal Accountant, should be submitted to the DepEd Central Office, with copies furnished to the concerned local sanggunian and the Regional Office of the Department of Budget and Management (DBM), not later than the 20th day after the end of each quarter for quarterly reports, and not later than the 15th day of February for annual reports.

The Municipal Accountant demands or follows up in writing the submission of liquidation reports from those barangays, SKs and leagues with unliquidated financial assistance, especially those that were granted in CY 2022 and prior years.

Monitor the compliance of  the winning bidders with the pre-requisite documents prior to contract signing as required in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

Municipal Treasurer  update its records and take initiative in the preparation of the required reports so as to properly recognize the amount of RPT and SET to be collected during the applicable year.

1.28 We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor order the:

GSO/Municipal Treasurer 

to maintain and update Property Cards regarding acquisition, description, custody,
disposal, and other information about the PPE, based on the source documents of the transactions, and reconcile with the Accounting Records and RPCPPE. 

Determine the PPE items comprising the 13 lumpsum amount of PPE recorded in the PCs.

Determine the person to whom the missing properties were issued. Issue a demand for the production, otherwise, the accountable officer should be asked to apply for relief of accountability following prescribed procedures/requirements.

Municipal Accountant:

To take action in the preparation and maintenance of the PPELC to record the acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal and other information about the property, plant and equipment, based on the source documents of the transactions.

Reconcile the balances of the PPE accounts in the General Ledgers and the PPELC.

Determine the PPE items comprising the 13 lumpsum amount of PPE recorded in the PPELC.

Prepare a journal entry for the derecognition upon disposition of the unserviceable and missing PPEs and for the recognition of the PPEs found at station.  

Inventory Committee, in coordination with the GSO and Municipal Accountant:

to complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period for one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.

Prepare a report on the result of the physical count through RPCPPE including the list of unserviceable PPEs, missing PPEs, and items found at station. 

Any discrepancy between the RPCPPE, PPELCs, and PCs shall be investigated, verified, and adjusted. The total reconciled balances for each PPE account shall be established as the correct balance of PPE at year-end;

to undertake the disposition of the non-existing PPEs and unserviceable PPEs in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.

2.14 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to prepare an adjusting entry to reclassify the recorded expenses to the appropriate PPE account with supporting documentation and to record the assets in the PPELC. 

2.15 We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to make a journal entry for
the reclassification of the completed project still recorded in Construction in
Progress to the appropriate Infrastructure Asset account. 

2.16 We finally recommended to recognize the corresponding depreciation of the
infrastructure assets from the date of completion as indicated in the Certificate
of Completion. The depreciation shall be for one month if the PPE is available
for use on or before the 15th of the month, and on the succeeding month if the
PPE is available for use after the 15th of the month.

3.14 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to:

a. record correctly the financial transactions charged to NGAs funds by
recognizing the asset purchased and appropriate expenses as it is incurred;

b. prepare the appropriate journal entries to reclassify the fire code fees,
PhilHealth Maternity Care Package and professional fees from the Due to
NGAs to Trust Liabilities account; and

c. immediately liquidate the fund transfers and return the unexpended
balance after the fulfillment of its conditions/purpose.

4.15 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
i. ensure that only the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE shall
be transferred to the Special Trust Fund at yearend, and

ii. prepare the appropriate accounting entries to record and correct
the recognition of financial transactions in the books of accounts in
accordance with the generally accepted accounting principles; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to transfer to the General Fund the excessive CY
2022 unspent balance credited to TF-DRRM, and refund of
P2,345,200.00 that should have been charged to the Special Trust Fund.

5.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

a. Require the deputy collectors to remit all collections daily and intact to the
Municipal Treasurer which should be covered by a certified Summary of
Collections and Remittances and supported by the duplicate copies of the
issued receipts; and

b. Issue a written authority deputizing the Barangay Treasurers to collect
Community Tax Certificate on her behalf. In the absence of said
authority, require the collectors to immediately turnover collections and
accountable forms to the Municipal Treasurer. Henceforth, no
accountable forms shall be issued to Barangay Treasurers without a
written authority as deputy collectors.

6.19 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the BAC Chairperson,
the following:

a. Require the submission of the latest Income Tax Return and Business
Tax Return within five (5) calendar days from receipt by the bidder of
the notice from the BAC that the bidder has the Lowest Calculated Bid
or Highest Rated Bid.

b. Carefully verify, validate, and ascertain all statements made in the
documents submitted by the bidder, and shall immediately notify the
said bidder in writing of its post-disqualification and the grounds for it,
if any, to declare as eligible only those who comply with the provisions
of the RIRR of RA 9184.

c. Submit justification why the contractors were awarded contracts
despite the non-submission of the latest Income Tax Return and
Business Tax Return and incomplete disclosure of the information of its
ongoing projects and the non-disclosure of other ongoing projects in the
Statement of All On-going Government and Private Contracts
Including Contracts Awarded but not yet Started

7.36 a. That the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Planning Development
Coordinator, Local Development Council, and Municipal Budget Officer,
identify specific projects to be funded from the 20% Development Fund
based on priority needs of the LGU. Also, stop lumpsum appropriations as
well as frequent augmentations, so that identified projects of the
Municipality will be implemented fully within the year.

b. That the Local Development Council prepare Annual Investment
Program that is realistic, well planned and implementation ready,
supported with program of works, and in harmony with the Annual
Budget.

c. That the Mayor secure approval or authority from the Sangguniang
Bayan prior to entering a contract in cases of lump sum appropriation
containing generic or general terms or authority to charge under the 20%
Development Fund.

7.37 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall not process the
disbursement of the projects under generic appropriation without authorization
from the Sangguniang Bayan that the Municipal Mayor may enter into contact
and to charge under the fund.

8.22 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Health
Officer:
i) issue a Special Order covering the assignment of public health workers
to hazardous/difficult areas,
ii) secure Certification by the Secretary of the DOH that the place of
assignment/travel is a strife-torn/embattled/disease-infested/distress or
isolated areas/stations, or areas declared under state of calamity or
emergency, or with volcano activity or eruption,
iii) ensure that Daily Time Records of RHU and Birthing Facility personnel
comply with CSC rules before signing the same, and
iv) attach the complete supporting documents to the disbursement
vouchers.

8.23 We likewise recommended the Municipal Accountant certify disbursement
vouchers that are supported with complete documentation and strictly observe
the guidelines on the grant of hazard pay, subsistence and laundry allowances.

8.24 We recommended for the refund of the overpayment received.

9.20 We recommended the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator (MPDC) that in the preparation of the Annual
Investment Plan (AIP), conduct a thorough review of the Peace and Order and
Public Safety (POPS) Plan of the LGU and ensure that the PPAs to be included
in the AIP are aligned with the priorities outlined in the POPS Plan and duly
supported by the Peace and Order Plan.

9.21 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local
Peace and Order Council include only those PPAs that are directly related to
crime prevention and law enforcement activities, capability development for
personnel of law enforcement agencies, volunteers/partners, or programs for
anti-illegal drug, illegal gambling, counter-insurgency, and/or counter-
terrorism efforts, among others that will directly contribute to the maintenance
of the peace and order and public safety.

9.22 We further recommended to appropriate Confidential Fund for the confidential
expenses of the Municipality, not exceeding 30% of the total POP. The release
and use of the CF shall be covered by a resolution duly approved by the LPOC
and the cash advances for the CF shall be liquidated within 30 days after every
quarter, or from the approved target date of completion, or after it has been
fully utilized, whichever comes first. The Liquidation Report shall be submitted
directly to the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Unit (ICFAU) in a
sealed envelope with a visible label "CONFIDENTIAL - For ICFAU Only,"
either through registered mail, courier, or an authorized liaison officer."

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer  expedite the search and retrieval of supporting documents and other relevant reports as a reference/proof in the reclassification of the Waterworks System project to its appropriate PPE account and consequently record its cumulative depreciation.
Municipal Accountant prepare necessary adjusting entries to recognize an impairment loss of the uncollectible accounts.

Ensure that prior to the receipt of any goods delivered, the necessary procurement procedures have already been completed and awarded to winning bidder/supplier and a contract of sale with the supplier has already been perfected; and

Comply with the procurement procedures by accomplishing the pertinent and relevant documents based on the guidelines and rules of procedures set forth in Republic Act No. 9184 and other applicable laws and regulations.

Reconcile the records and prepare the corresponding journal entries to record the receipt and utilization of the donated items and the remaining inventory.

Ask for the unit costs of the supplies not provided with such data from source agencies for recording in the books.
Strictly observe the provisions of EO No. 77 in future travels to avoid disallowance in audit.
Municipal Accountant prepare necessary adjusting entries to recognize impairment of the uncollectible accounts.

Disallow the practice of contract signing with winning bidders in the absence of the required performance security to protect the interest of the Municipality.

Submit a valid justification why the procedural guidelines that should be followed  after failure of the winning bidder to post the performance security within the period stipulated in the RIRR were not complied with.

Submit explanation for  the inclusion of ineligible families who received the SAP cash assistance.

Initiate the collection  for the refund by sending demand letters.

Require the ineligible beneficiaries to refund the cash aid they received and ensure that the total amount of ₱726,000 is  collected and returned to DSWD, and submit to COA proof of compliance thereof.

Obtain the lacking photocopies of ID cards, or in their  absence, a Barangay Certification using the prescribed format in Annex A of the Memorandum Circular, from the beneficiaries and submit them to the Audit Team.

Proceed with the issuance of the notices.

Enforcement of such collections and remedies as provided for in Republic Act 7160 in order to recoup the unpaid taxes due to the Municipality.

Municipal Treasurer  deposit his collections intact daily in conformity with Section 89 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units Volume I to safeguard government funds from risk of loss.

Cash advance for salaries should also be submitted together with a copy of the payrolls, even though they are still without the signatures of the payees, to show that the amount of the cash advance is equal to the net payroll for a pay period, as required in Section 175 of the GAAM.

Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to locate and submit immediately the missing disbursement vouchers/checks, with all the supporting documents, so that these can be verified.

1.18 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint a qualified person to be
the General Services Officer who will: (a) take custody of and be accountable
for all properties, real or personal, owned by the local government unit and
those granted to it in the form of donation, reparation, assistance and
counterpart of joint projects, and (b) shall maintain and update the records
on the acquisition, description, custody, disposal and other information about
the property, plant and equipment based on the source documents of the
transactions in the Property Cards;
1.19 Further, we recommended the Municipal Accountant take action in the
preparation and maintenance of the PPELC to record the acquisition,
description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal and other
information about the property, plant and equipment, based on the source
documents of the transactions and reconcile the property records with the
RPCPPE in accordance with the provisions of Section 6.3 of COA Circular
No. 2020-006.
1.20 We also recommended that the Inventory Committee formulate a schedule for the
conduct of physical inventory that will complement or balance with the time of
the members of the committee in order not to prejudice the operation of the
Municipality. Also, conduct the physical count pursuant to 6.1 and 6.2 of COA
Circular No. 2020-006.

2.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to:
(1) prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the completed projects from the
CIP to the appropriate PPE account, and henceforth effect the reclassification
of the completed projects upon completion as shown in the Certificate of
Completion; and
(2) recognize depreciation on the PPE from the date of completion.
Depreciation shall be for one month if the PPE is available for use on or
before the 15th of the month, and on the succeeding month if the PPE is
available for use after the 15th of the month.

3.15 We recommended the Municipal Mayor execute a Lease Contract with the tenants
specifying therewith the unit, monthly rental, terms, and penalty for late payment.
Also, we recommended for the Mayor to direct the:
1. MTO to secure a file copy of the Lease Contract and furnish copy to the
Municipal Accountant. Also, provide a collection monitoring record per tenant
to determine whether payments were made on time or there were uncollected
outstanding balances.
2. Municipal Accountant to record the income on an accrual basis and prepare
subsidiary ledger per tenant. Also, conduct monthly reconciliation with the
MTO records.

4.18 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a) trace prior years balances of accounts payable and the correctness of
accounting entries on its recognition and settlement, and make the appropriate
journal entry, as necessary,
b) maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for each payee/creditor which should be
reconciled with the balance in the General Ledger,
c) stop the practice of recording accounts payable without complete
supporting documents. Henceforth, JEV on the recognition of payables should
be supported with complete documents to prove validity, correctness, and
existence of the claims.

4.19 We also recommended the Municipal Budget Officer and Treasurer to
coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in the tracing of obligated and
settled liabilities of the Municipality.

5.12 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Reconcile book records with that of the lending institutions, trace prior
year journal entries on the payment of the loans and effect the necessary
adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the account; and
b) Maintain subsidiary ledgers for all loans payable per purpose of the loan for
check and balance, and aid in tracing of transactions and monitoring of loan
balances.

6.9 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with the provisions
of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and exercise due diligence in the
processing of claims and complete documentation must be observed
before effecting payment.
b. Require the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to submit
the lacking documents listed in Table 1 of this AOM, except for As-
Built Plans which were already submitted to the COA Auditor. This is
without prejudice to the issuance of notice of suspension.

7.6 We recommended the Municipal Mayor, through the concerned offices, to
ensure the timely project and program implementation, and turnover of the
same should be properly documented and made to authorized beneficiaries as
indicated in the Memorandum of Agreement with the source agency.

7.7 We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to refrain from processing/
signing disbursement vouchers and liquidation reports with incomplete
supporting documents.

7.8 Also, submit the copy of the MOAs we requested covering the fund transfers
received from the Source Agency which is the guidelines followed by LGU in
the implementation of PPAs.

8.19 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. Require the Budget Officer to prepare a supplemental budget to be
authorized through the enactment of Appropriation Ordinance by the
Sangguniang Bayan whenever the change in the Annual Budget is covered
under Section 321 of Republic Act No. 7160 and attached therewith
certificate of availability of fund under oath the local treasurer and local
accountant and attested by the local chief executive.
2. Require the MDRRMO to annually prepare the LDRRMFIP of all
identified LDRRMF PPAs, and Revised LDRRMFIP to incorporate
realigned PPAs. The LDRRMFIP shall be approved by the MDRRM Council.
3. Require the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to timely update
the availability of the fund to determine at year-end the unexpended
balance of the MOOE and QRF to be transferred to STF-DRRM Fund.

9.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Local Development
Council, identify specific projects to be funded from the 20% Development
Fund based on priority needs of the LGU, and to prepare Annual Investment
Program to be more realistic, well planned and implementation ready.

9.14 We also recommended that the Budget Officer, as far as practicable, to finance
all the PPAs identified in the AIP by the Local Development Council to
harmonize the Annual Budget with the AIP.

10.17 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Planning
Development Coordinator, Local Development Council, and Municipal
Budget Officer to prepare the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget
specifying therewith the Programs/Projects/Activities to be implemented and
to avoid lump sum appropriation containing generic or general terms.

10.18 We also recommended that the Mayor, through the Bids and Awards
Committee, secure approval or authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to
enter into contract prior to entering a contract with a winning bidder in cases
of lump sum appropriation containing generic or general terms or authority
to charge under the 20% Development Fund.

11.15 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a.) Require the HRMO to assess the existing Organizational Structure so
that the ideal number of plantilla personnel will be established and
submit a report with recommendations thereon to the Local Chief
Executive;
b.) Create clear-cut policies and guidelines to address the issue on the
necessity and propriety of hiring JOs taking into consideration the
existing regular employees;
c.) Comply with the existing daily minimum wage rate for Region VIII, as
prescribed by the RTWPB;
d.) Require the Municipal Accountant to process payrolls with complete
documentary requirements and with the complete signatures of the
authorized signatories of the payroll.

12.20 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the collectors to remit the
collections daily before 5:00 PM or before leaving the office in case of
emergency leave, and direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all her
collections, as well as collections turned over by the Revenue Collecting Clerks,
daily or not later than the next banking day.

12.21 We further recommended to submit a request for relief of accountability
together the supporting documents and justification for the late submission of
request.

12.22 Henceforth, we recommended to ensure that the Fidelity Bond of the
Accountable Officers will be sufficient to cover their total accountabilities.

12.23 In their reply, the Municipal Treasurer commented that she already sent a
memorandum letter to all revenue collectors for the daily turnover of their
collections. Likewise, sufficiency of the fidelity bond to cover the Accountable
Officer’s accountability shall be observed.

13.24 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Public Health Nurse
and the Municipal Health Officer as Vaccine Coordinators to:
1. Submit to the Audit Team the revised report of the total vaccines received,
and the lacking documentation relative to the receipt and distribution of
the covid vaccines and ancillary supplies such as: inspection and
acceptance reports, distribution reports, master list of vaccinated
individuals, incident reports and Inventory of Vaccines as of December 31,
2022;
2. To develop and/or enhance measures that will provide for the swift
administration of any vaccines to eligible population such as but not
limited to putting premium on communications and advocacy to boost the
confidence and acceptance of the vaccine; and
3. Develop distribution plan or strategy appropriate to their situation,
including inspection process prior to acceptance of vaccines and timelines
to avoid incidence of expiration of vaccines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to prioritize the compliance with COA Circular 2020-006 for the cleansing of PPE accounts. We enjoined the Municipal Mayor to create a new Inventory Committee who shall be temporarily relieved of their regular duties to complete the physical inventory in three months or less. The Inventory Committee shall prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities, and shall ensure its full implementation.
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the proposed adjusting entries as follows:

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to recognize impairment of the uncollectible accounts. Thereafter, Management shall process derecognition of dormant receivable accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 by filing a request for write-off to be supported by certified relevant documents validating the reason for non-collectability.

We recommended and BAC agreed to submit a valid justification on the noted deviation from the implementing rules and regulations for competitive public bidding. Henceforth, the BAC shall ensure that the LCB will undergo post-qualification to determine responsiveness of the bid before acting for recommendation of award to the HoPE, to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to submit justification why the OSS was not verified as to completeness of mandatory provisions during the post-qualification stage. Henceforth, the BAC shall ensure that the OSS executed by bidders or its authorized representatives contain all the required provisions to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to disallow the practice of contract signing with winning bidders in the absence of the required performance security to protect the interest of the Municipality. We recommended further that the HoPE’s office will take charge in requiring the posting of performance security by the winning bidders instead of the BAC.  Also, the BAC is enjoined to submit a valid justification why the procedural guidelines that should be acted after failure of the winning bidder to post the performance security within the period stipulated in the RIRR were not complied with.
We recommended and Management agreed to submit an acceptable explanation/justification on the noted excessiveness of the contract price over the COA cost estimate to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to establish implementing guidelines on the grant of assistance to individuals/family in crisis situation which will clearly set the qualification of the indigent, amount and frequency of assistance, documentary requirements and the like, concurred by the Sangguniang Bayan, to provide proper control in the disbursement of public funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to coordinate with NTC to work out on other legal procedures to authorize the purchase and use of the radio communication devices in the absence of the purchaser’s and dealer’s permits.

1. The General Services Officer/ Municipal Treasurer maintain and update
the records regarding the acquisition, description, custody, disposal and
other information about the property, plant and equipment based on the source
documents of the transactions in the Property Cards.
2. The Municipal Accountant (a) maintain a Property, Plant and
Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELCs) to record the acquisition, estimated life, rate of
depreciation, disposal, and other information of PPE based on the source
documents of the transactions; and (b) recognize depreciation expense for
each item of PPE; (c) determine the accumulated depreciation per item of the
PPEs, and (d) draw an adjusting entry to transfer the accumulated depreciation
of PPEs that were reclassified.
3. Inventory Committee conduct a physical count of all the properties of the
Municipality and submit the RCPPE as of yearend pursuant to 6.1 and 6.2 of
COA Circular No. 2020-006, and temporarily relieved the personnel who will
conduct the physical count from their daily task to devote their full time in
the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed.
4. Compute the depreciation of all undepreciated assets and draw a journal entry
to set the accounts in order.
We also recommended that the GSO or the Local Treasurer, in coordination with
the Accounting Department to:
a. Reconcile the property records with that of the RPCPPE in accordance
with the provisions of Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and
after completing the reconciliation procedures, the total reconciled
balances for each PPE account shall be established as the correct balance
of PPE at year end;
b. Dispose the non-existing/missing PPEs in accordance with Section 7 of
COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
c. prepare, maintain and update all the necessary monitoring records such
as the PCs and PPELCs respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant give utmost priority in the
preparation and timely submission of the Monthly Bank Reconciliation
Statement on all bank accounts of the Municipality in order to identify all
differences existing between its records and depository banks, detect errors
immediately; and prepare the necessary adjustments in the books on all
reconciling items, where appropriate;

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant (1) Restore unreleased
checks by preparing a journal entry voucher at the end of the year based on
the Schedule submitted from the Municipal Treasury Office, and (2) at the
start of the ensuing year, draw another entry to reverse the previous entry
made and recognize the availability of the checks for release.

We recommended that the Supply Officer and Accounting Unit personnel
familiarize with the rules and procedures on supply management and its
accounting procedure and attend a seminar on supply management.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
1. The Supply Officer to prepare a Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) every time
supplies and materials are issued, summarize the RIS in a Summary of
Supplies and Material Issued (SSMI), and to submit the SSMI supported
with all RIS to the Accountant;
2. The Office of the Municipal Agriculturist to submit to the Accountant the
Acknowledgement Receipts of all inventories distributed by them to farmer
beneficiaries;
3. The Accountant to:
a) Record purchased inventory to the appropriate asset account and
issuances to the expense account,
b) record inventories only as expense based on the submitted SSMI,
c) refrain from posting to the General Ledger without recording a
journal entry in the Journals, and
d) submit the General Journals with complete supporting documents
to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Accountant ensure correct accounts are used in
recording transactionns. Make the following correcting journal entries:
CIP-Land Improvements 1,040,389.35
Prior Period Adjustments 884,330.95
Advances to Contractors 156,058.40
Other Land Improvements 1,040,389.35
CIP-Land Improvements 1,040,389.35

In order to comply with accounting standards in Borrowing Cost, we
recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments
to capitalize the interests on loans as part of the cost of the asset that was
recorded as interest expenses. The Borrowing Cost should be identified per
project to which it was capitalized.

Dr. Construction in Progress P483,198.80
Cr. Prior Period Adjustments P483,198.80

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant (1) apply consistently
the accounting standards in Borrowing Cost; (2) commence the capitalization
of the Borrowing only when the conditions prescribed under Item 31 of IPSAS
5 are met and cease the recognition when substantially all the activities
necessary to prepare the qualifying asset for its intended use or sale are
complete; (3) Lastly, provide disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statement
of the amount of borrowing cost capitalized for the period.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor strictly enforce the posting of the
warranty security by the contractors after the issuance of the Certificate of
Final Acceptance by the Municipality pursuant to the provisions of the
Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

Also, we recommended to the Municipal Accountant comply with the
documentary requirements set under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and conduct
a pre-audit in the processing of claims and complete documentation must be
observed before effecting payment, and to submit the lacking documents.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the officials concerned
to monitor the delivery of every procurement and demand delivery within
the period prescribed under the contract to avoid delays.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
1. Maintain a Subsidiary Ledger for every fund transfer, and practice
adequate record-keeping and documentation;.
2. Monitor and conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and
validation of the existence of the fund transfers;
3. Determine the Source Agency and determine if the Municipality has
existing valid payables from the source agencies, confirm continuity of the
programs/projects; and
4. Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers, and liquidation of the balances.

Finally, return the unspent balance of the Integrated Community Food
Productions amounting to P58,259.00 to the Source Agency, since it has
already been long outstanding.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Local Development
Council, identify specific projects to be funded from the 20% Economic
Development Fund based on priority needs of the LGU. Also, stop lumpsum
appropriations as well as frequent augmentations, so that identified projects of the
Municipality will be implemented fully within the year.

We also recommended that the Mayor, through the Bids and Awards
Committee, secure approval or authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod
to enter into contract prior to entering a contract with a winning bidder in
cases of lump sum appropriation containing generic or general terms or
authority to charge under the 20% Development Fund.

We also recommended the Local Development Council prepare Annual
Investment Program that is realistic, well planned and implementation ready
and in harmony with the Annual Budget.

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal
Treasurer make justification on the procurement of relief goods for the victims of
Typhoon Odette charged to 20% Development Fund. This is without
prejudiced to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan specifiy the fund source set
aside for the new PPAs in their resolutions, and the Municipal Mayor to
direct:

a. The MDRRMO to prepare annually an LDRRMFIP of all identified
LDRRMF PPAs and Revised LDRRMFIP to incorporate re-aligned
PPAs. The LDRRMFIP shall also include PPAs charged against the PY
unexpended LDRRMF;

b. The Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to regularly reconcile
their records to determine the remaining available balance, transfer at
year end any unexpended current year LDRRMF to the Trust Fund, and
revert to the General Fund any PY unexpended balances that are beyond
five years;

c. The Budget Officer to maintain the RAAO and annually furnish the
Audit Team for verification purposes; and

d. The Accountant to:
1. include in the LDRRM Fund Utilization Report the available and
utilized PY balances;
2. maintain subsidiary ledger of the Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund account by year of transfer of
the unexpended LDRRMF; and
3. Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the amount and details
of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF.

In order to establish the regularity of the grant of the AHDP/SRA, we
recommended that the Rural Health Unit personnel and other office
concerned submit lacking documents/ records/ orders to the Audit Team for
payment of SRA and AHDP; and accordingly observe compliance on the
documentary requirements for these kinds of personnel incentives to avoid
suspension in audit;

We also recommended the Municipal Accountant ensure all necessary
supporting documents are attached to claims before approving all payments.
Henceforth, only disbursement vouchers/payrolls with complete supporting
documents should be processed for payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the collectors to remit the
collections daily before 5:00 PM or before leaving the office in case of
emergency leave, and direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all her
collections, as well as collections turned over by the Revenue Collecting Clerks,
daily or not later than the next banking day.

We recommended that the Municipal Social and Development Officer together
with the Property Supply Officer:

a. Conduct a physical count of all the remaining donated items.

b. Prepare an inventory list of the remaining donated items for DRRM every
month or every quarter or until all donations are fully distributed;

c. Henceforth, prepare all the reports and documents required to account
and monitor the receipt and issuances of all donations in kind and submit a
copy of all the reports to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Supply Officer furnish the Municipal
Accountant with the duplicate copies of all Acknowledgment Receipts of in-
kind donations, invoice receipts, and an inventory list of donated items for DRRM.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant summarize data from
the documents submitted by the Municipal Social and Development Officer
and Supply Officer compare records with the Registry of Donated Relief
Goods for DRRM maintained by the Property and Supply Unit for check and
balance, and disclose the necessary information in the Notes to the Financial
Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
1. Trace prior years’ accounting entries and conduct a thorough review of
the affected accounts to ascertain the cause of the negative balances and
initiate necessary corrections/adjustments.
2. Maintain adequate documentation and monitoring to avoid the occurrence
of negative balances.
3. Make an accounting on the balance of all transfers in the Trust Fund for
Community Tax and Fire Inspection Fees.

Also, we recommended that the Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:
4. Stop the transfer from the General Fund to Trust Fund for the share of
Community Tax and Fire Inspection Fees;
5. Remittance for the share of the Barangay as well as the utilization of the
BFP share shall be made in the General Fund; and
6. Draw a check to revert back to the General Fund based on the balance
determined by the Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
1.18 We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees one
month before the checks become stale. Stale checks shall be reported and
attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting
entries for the stale checks over 6 months but not over 2 years amounting to
P290.20, unrecorded deposit of P77,935.35, unrecorded bank credit memo
of P191,427.43 and unrecorded interest earned of P3,212.05, the
recommended accounting entries are as follows:


(*to represent surplus adjustment)

c. Require the Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare and submit bank
reconciliation statements for all bank accounts of the agency to the Auditor
and observe the prescribed period in the submission of monthly bank
reconciliation statements pursuant to COA Circualr No. 96-011 and draw
the entry to records in the books the LCCA maintained for the
implementation of REDD+ program with a bank balance of P23,863.30.
d. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Subsidiary
Ledger Cards for the Cash in Bank-LCCA Current Account. containing
complete significant information and history of the transactions related to
the said account.
e. Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a Cashbook for Cash in Bank
for nine (9) bank accounts and shall be used to record deposits of collections
and withdrawls from the bank thru issuance of checks. The cashbook shall
be maintained by bank account and by fund.

2.19 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Require the Inventory Team to complete the physical count of all properties
as at year-end and prepare and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31
of the following year to the Audit Team and periodically conduct physical
count of PPEs which should be done annually;
b. Require the GSO/MTO to determine to whom the missing properties were
issued. He shall then issue a demand for the production, otherwise, the
accountable officer should be asked to apply for relief of accountability
following prescribed procedures/requirements and require the Municipal
Accountant to derecognized from the books.
c. Require the Municipal Accountant and GSO/MTO to exert effort to
determine the composition of PPE adjustments recorded in lump amount.
d. Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to
correct misclassification PPEs and erroneous recording of non-PPE items.
e. Direct the GSO/MTO to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete
and unserviceable properties. Also, once proper disposal has been
undertaken, the Municipal Accountant should derecognize in the books said
unserviceable PPE items.
f. Require the Accountant to prepare journal entries for the transfer of PPEs
under the Trust Fund to the General Fund.

3.14 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventories at
least once a year and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories
(RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to record all regularly procured supplies
and materials to the appropriate inventory accounts following the perpetual
inventory method;
c. Require the GSO to maintain Supplies Cards (SCs) for supplies and materials
by type/class and prepare and submit the SSMI based on consolidated RIS to
the Municipal Accountant within five days after the end of the month and the
SSMI shall be the basis in drawing a JV to record the issuance; and
d. Require the Municipal Accountant to draw the JV based on the SSMI to
record the issuance.

4.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant-
Designate to trace the period or date of acquisition of the various PPEs that have
not yet been provided with depreciation and compute the depreciation based on
the estimated useful life of assets as provided under COA Circular 2017-004
dated December 13, 2017 and DPWH Department Order No. 176 series of 2015
for Infrastructure Assets. Prior years' depreciation should be taken up as Prior
Years Adjustment to be closed to Government Equity and current year's
depreciation should be recorded as expense. Moreover, depreciation should also
be provided for calendar years 2018 and 2019 on all PPEs.

5.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all the Revenue Collection
Clerks to remit to the Municipal Treasurer the full amount of their collections on
a daily basis regardless of the nature of collections made.

5.9 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her
collections, as well as collections turned over to her by the Revenue Collecting
Clerks, daily or not later than the next banking day.

6.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Comply strictly with Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002
by requiring the liquidation of cash advances of officers and employees with
unliquidated cash advances as soon as the purpose it was given was served.
b. Enforce settlement of outstanding cash advances by sending demand letters
to concerned officials and employees with unsettled cash advances,
otherwise effect the withholding of salaries and claims of those who failed
to settle despite demand.
c. Stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to employees unless
the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or proper accounting
thereof is made.
d. Issue a certification that the employee has no outstanding cash advance
before granting new cash advances.
e. Request the write-off of the unliquidated cash advance balances of the
deceased employees.

7.24 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) To require the BAC to be updated with the related issuance of GPPB.
b) To require the Municipal Accountant to create a Special Account in the
General Fund for the BGCM Fund pursuant to Section 313 of R.A. 7160.
c) To require all the personnel concerned, especially the Municipal
Accountant, to secure complete documentation on all disbursements, and to
ensure the propriety of the transactions. We also recommend for the
submission of the lacking supporting documents for the aforementioned
transactions. This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of
Suspension as a result of our audit.
d) To require the GSO in charge and the authorized Inspection Officer to fill
out the date and necessary details of the Inspection and Acceptance Report,
and to assure that the items received are in accordance with the contract.

8.12 We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Direct the MDRRMO and MSWDO to submit the complete list of recipients
of the distribution of welfare goods for verification indicating therein the type
and quantity of items received per beneficiary. The list must bear complete
signatures of the receipts of welfare goods;
b. Direct the MDRRMO, Municipal Health Officer (MHO), and other
departments in-charge of the quarantine facility to submit the Attendance
Sheet/List of quarantined PUIs, PUMs, LSIs and OFWs duly certified by the
MHO with details on date of arrival and release from the facility; and
c. Direct the MHO to submit also the list of frontliners on duty detailing the
work schedule with their signature as proof of attendance and receipt of
meals packs.

9.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor must:
a. Require the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan to enact an Ordinance for
the realignment of funds and a supplemental budget for any changes in the
Annual Budget to cover new PPAs from realigned funds or available funds
from new revenue source as certified by the Municipal Treasurer.
b. Require the members of the Local Development Council and Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to prepare a supplemental
AIP to support the changes in the Annual Budget.
c. Require the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant, to process
payment for the utilization of the LDRRMF only for programs, projects and
activities which are included in the LDRRMF Investment Plan or those
included in the approved Supplemental AIP to ensure legality of the
transactions.

10.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor must:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to make sure of the complete
documentation on all disbursements, and to ensure the propriety of the
transactions. She must submit the above lacking supporting documents.
b. Require the GSO in charge and the Inspection Officer to complete the
details of the AIR and make sure that the items delivered are in compliance
with the contract/PO.

11.20 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to issue an Official Receipt for all cash
donation received.
b. Direct his authorized representative to issue Acknowledgement Receipt for
the receipt of in-kind donations.
c. Direct the General Service Officer (GSO) to record the delivered items in
Supply Ledger Card (SLC), released supplies supported with Requisition
and Issue Slip (RIS) and record issuance in the SLC, and consolidate the RIS
in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) and forward to
Accounting Unit.
d. Require the GSO together with the members of Inventory Committee to
include In-kind Donations received by the Municipality in the physical
count and Inventory Report.
e. Submit the report on the receipt, distributions, and balances for in-kind
donations and the report of receipt and utilization of cash donations together
with their supporting documents as a special report to the COA Auditor.

12.16 We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor must prioritize the re-channeling of funds to other
development projects if the approved PPAs could no longer be
implemented due to causes not attributable to the fault or negligence of the
Municipality and direct the Municipal Engineer and MPDO to the fast-track
and monitor its implementation.
b. Require the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan to enact a supplemental
budget for any changes in the Annual Budget to cover new PPAs from
realigned funds or new available funds from new revenue source as
certified by the Municipal Treasurer.
c. Require the members of the Local Development Council and Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to prepare a
supplemental AIP to support the changes in the Annual budget.

13.15 We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Engineer must submit inspection report of the implemented
projects that will quantify the work actually done. Payments for various
projects must be substantiated with pictures taken during actual project
implementation;
b. The Municipal Engineer should prepare and submit the detailed Program
of Work and quantified output indicated and with realistic cost estimates
that would maximize the funds allocated for a specific project, complete
with the plans and drawings as warranted; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to verify completeness of data in the
supporting documents attached to disbursements vouchers such as
Purchase Requests, Purchase Orders, and Inspection & Acceptance
Reports, etc. before these are forwarded to the Municipal Treasurer for
payment and make sure of the complete documentation on all
disbursements, and to ensure the propriety of the transactions. She must
submit the above lacking supporting documents.

14.11 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and certify a monthly Report
on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF.
b. Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer
(LDRRMO) to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on
or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMF and
LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA Auditor for
verification.
c. Provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to FS for amount and details of the
unexpended balance of LDRRMF.

15.9 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 and exercise due diligence in the processing of claims
and complete documentation must be observed before effecting payment.
b. Direct the BAC Members to comply with the standard cost of bidding
documents as prescribed in Appendix 8 (Guidelines on the sale of Bidding
Documents) of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the BAC Secretariat to submit the
lacking documents to COA Auditor for evaluation without prejudice on the
issuance of notice of suspension.
We recommended that Management provide the Audit Team with the duly received copy of the one-time report for donations submitted to the NDRRMC through the OCD.

We recommended that Management submit justification why no efforts had been made to find GPPB issuances and advisories on the manner of posting in the GPPB online portal of all the required information relative to the awarded Emergency Procurement projects under the Bayanihan Act.

We recommended that the Municipality submit justification for undertaking the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act without complying with the requirements of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020, especially in the non-inclusion of the required warranty clause in the contracts/purchase orders. We recommended that the Municipal Engineer ensure the submission of the As-Built Plans for the subject completed infrastructure projects totaling ₱46,297,445.45. Henceforth, As-Built Plans shall be required by the Municipal Accountant in the processing of final payments for all completed infrastructure projects.

We recommended that Management prospectively ensure that grants of financial assistance with specific purpose to the barangays are duly supported with MOA and the Municipal Accountant’s certification.

We recommended that Management direct the responsible officials to obtain the lacking photocopies of ID cards, or in its absence, a Barangay Certification using the prescribed format in Annex A of the Memorandum Circular, from the beneficiaries and submit to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the affected accounts to correct the understatement of depreciation expenses and accumulated depreciation. A copy of the journal entry voucher (JEV) shall be provided to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management submit explanation on the inclusion of ineligible families who received the SAP cash assistance. We recommended further to require the ineligible beneficiaries to refund the cash aid they received and ensure that the total amount of ₱726,000 are collected and returned to DSWD, and submit to COA proof of compliance thereof. We enjoined the responsible officials to initiate the collection efforts for the refund by sending demand letters.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to book up all public infrastructures that were transferred to the Registry of Public Infrastructure as part of Property, Plant and Equipment account for a fair presentation of the financial statements.
