observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that conditions for the preliminary commencement of construction have been addressed and that a certification shall be executed that necessary requirements and documents have been undertaken for the project to be completed as planned.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to send billings to collect unpaid liquidated damages or may collect such liquidated damages from other securities posted by them, pursuant to regulations. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to rescind the contract and to impose appropriate sanctions on contracts whose liquidated damages exceeded the 10% ceiling per RIRR of RA No. 9184 to address continuing delay in the completion of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) to direct the concerned officials to limit the grant of overtime services only when necessary and within its appropriation, and b) to direct the Municipal Accountant to make sure that overtime services have proper prior authorization and that overtime claims are supported with quantified accomplishment report.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the Local Finance Committee to see to it that budget is lodged in reasonably projected and collectible for the ensuing fiscal year and that appropriate support for the budget be provided.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the GAD Focal Person to adhere to the provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-001 to ensure that the PPAs included in the GAD Plan and Budget address valid GAD issues identified through the conduct of gender analysis.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to ensure full settlement of the remaining disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the applicable tax rate from (1) honoraria to resource speakers on different training and seminars, (2) honoraria of consultants of different projects, (3) honoraria of personnel of national offices assigned in the Municipality, (4) salaries and wages of PRDP personnel designated as members of BAC and oversight committee of the implementation of PRDP projects.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the local sanggunian to: a) craft ordinances and issue executive orders related to waste management and environmental protection, b) add more waste recycling facilities, c) improve garbage classification, collection and recycling, d) explore recycling methods of the recyclables.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. to send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder with the signature of the Mayor or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and employees concerned to settle their cash advance; b. not to sign claims for additional cash advance until the previous cash advance is settled; c. to cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed period after being sent notice and demand; d. to furnish all department heads listings of all unliquidated cash advances of employees under their charge to enhance efforts to effect liquidation.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct the Municipal Agricultural Officer together with the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of the animals and breeding stocks; b. require the MAO to conduct thorough analysis/investigation on those recipients who refunded/sold the animals assigned/dispersed to them; c. require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the work, other animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Card.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that only projects aligned with the purpose/objectives of the 20% Development Fund be funded from such appropriation. The Municipal Accountant should see to it that expenses charged against 20% DF is in accordance with prescribed guidelines.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer, MPDC, Municipal Agricultural Officer, and Municipal Health Officer to fast-track the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities funded by the BUB fund and LGSF to provide constituents the benefits.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to collect from the contractor the said advance payment for mobilization, and properly document the collection.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the HRMO to adhere to the provisions of Section 77 of RA No. 7160 which provides that the Municipality may hire Job-order employees only for local projects authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to ensure that programs, projects and activities are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection and other assistance that may be provided.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in enforcing settlement of audit disallowances; ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from signing cash advance vouchers and obligation request of officials and employees with outstanding cash advances and enforce liquidation.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the officers and employees to refund the amounts collected in the absence of legal basis.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) cause the submission of justification why the 20% development fund was utilized for expenditures not in consonance with DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2017; and (b) ensure that appropriations are used only for eligible PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts of the LGU the medical equipment received from the Department of Health, Regional Office No. VIII to reflect the accurate asset account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to fast-track the implementation of the programs and projects funded by the National Government.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) as required under Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and as prescribed by GPPB.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive impose on the Municipal Accountant the timely submission of accounts to COA to comply with the provisions of Sec. 100 and 107 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to revisit LBC Cir. No. 103 and COA Cir. No. 2012-001 in order to be guided in the processing of claims for RATA.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to intensify collection efforts and pursue remedies provided under the Local Government Code such as auction and distraint; undertake information campaigns and publish lists of delinquent properties.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Vice Mayor exercise prudence in the use of government funds: 1) seminars/training where participants come from the province should be conducted within the province; 2) send participants only to seminars within their area of concern; 3) make representation with the PCL to conform with regulations; and 4) ensure documentary requirements such as Activity Design and Post Activity Report be attached.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should (1) strictly comply with the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and Section 4.1.3, 5.1.1 and 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and EO No. 298 dated March 23, 2004; (2) monitor closely/follow-up the liquidation; (3) require Accountable Officers to liquidate to avoid withholding of salary and sanctions.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record all financial assistance with specific purpose granted to Barangays initially to Due from LGUs and recognize expense only during liquidation by debiting to Subsidy to Other LGUs.
observations.managementAction: In 2020, all financial assistance given to the barangays were recorded by the Municipal Accountant under the account of Due from LGUs and then, as subsidy to other LGUs once liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through the monitoring and assessment of the Municipal Engineer, ensure that the defects liability period or one year from date of completion has lapsed before finally issuing the Certificate of Final Acceptance, to guarantee that defects in the construction works within this period are rectified at the contractor’s expense. Moreover, we enjoined the Municipal Engineer to submit copies of the Certificate of Final Acceptance for the subject completed projects if such documents were actually issued.
observations.managementAction: In 2020, the Certificate of Final Acceptance was not issued but instead a Certificate of Completion or Accomplishment Report (100%) signed by the Municipal Engineer as support that project was completed already.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor follow the provisions of Section 219 of RA 7160 and undertake the procedures for the general revision of real property assessments. Prospectively, regular general revision for every three years as required by the law shall be undertaken.
observations.managementAction:
The revision of the LGU real property assessment is under the authority and responsibility of the Cebu Provincial Government and the LGU is only acting as collecting agent. 

But in 2020, the Cebu Provincial Government already effected the new Real Property Tax Assessment.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the provisions of Sec. 5.1.3 of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 2007 be continually applied, that the local chief executive should order the withholding of the salaries and any money due to these officials and employees with unsettled cash advances and no cash advance shall be granted to them unless these overdue cash advances are settled or liquidated.
observations.managementAction: Cash advance balances granted prior to CY 2011 were already settled except for those who were separated without clearance and deceased employees. For those separated without clearance, the balance will be deducted from its available leave credits monetization.

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Accountant to comply with the deficiencies detailed in the attached schedule. Stop the practice of issuing and signing checks even without the supporting papers. Henceforth, all disbursement vouchers should bear complete documentation before these are submitted to the COA for verification.
observations.managementAction:
The 378 DVs which pertains to TEV of employees’ lacking bus tickets and taxi tickets would be impracticable to provide due to passage of time and non-availability of documents required. 

However,  management is now careful on the TEV claims by setting limit on those unreceipted fare based on the customary mode of transportation and in compliance with COA circular on the increased amount to ₱300.00 for those expenses not requiring receipt.

The Management is asking the COA for consideration to close this finding due to impracticality of submitting the source documents required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the bookkeeper stop using the estimated amounts in recording the consumption/use of the inventory items but require the use of RIS.  Likewise, direct the Supply Officer to summarize the RIS monthly and submit to the Accountant for recording in the books.  Also, require the conduct of physical count of all inventory items semi-annually for monitoring and control
observations.managementAction: The LGU did not practice stocking of supplies due to limited space in the GSO. Once the items arrived/delivered, GSO will issue to the end user hence inventory were expensed upon issuance to end user.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned BAC personnel submit justification on why the excess honoraria paid to them shall not be disallowed in audit. Prospectively, LGU shall observe the specific guidelines in the proper computation of honoraria for personnel involved in government procurement as provided in the Revised IRR of RA 9184 particularly in the observance of its maximum rates and limit.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC opted for the 25% of monthly basic salary per month as BAC honorarium ceiling and overlooked the rate per awarded contract. 

Furthermore in CY 2020, the BAC honorarium is based on per project already awarded with a close monitoring that honorarium received will not exceed the maximum limit of 25% of their basic salary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the officers concerned be required to submit a written and satisfactory justification for the option made, otherwise direct the persons who attended the seminars to refund the amounts in excess of the allowable travel expenses for a seminar in Cebu City. Henceforth, management should see to it that all resources of the government shall be managed, expended or utilized in accordance with law and regulations, and safeguarded against loss or wastage through illegal or improper disposition with a view to ensuing efficiency, economy and effectiveness in government operations.
observations.managementAction: In 2020 and due to Covid-19 pandemic, all travels during the ECQ/MECQ were cancelled/ prohibited. Furthermore, the management is already careful in granting travel authority to those travels with Cebu City venue, unless necessary and with justifiable reason.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts under the three funds and book the necessary adjustments to bring their balances into agreement.  Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should closely monitor transactions affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure that these are recorded in the different funds simultaneously to avoid any error of omission.  Moreover, whenever possible, settle immediately transactions between funds to minimize efforts in monitoring and reconciliation and more importantly avoid erroneous account balances.
observations.managementAction:
An adjusting entry was already made by the previous accountant.

In CY 2020, reciprocal accounts were closely monitored and reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer proceed with the issuance of the notices, the posting thereof and the prospective enforcement of such collections and remedies as provided for in Republic Act 7160 in order to recoup the unpaid taxes due to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Assessor is doing its best to locate and inform the owners of these delinquent RPT by upgrading their iTAX system.
observations.reason: There still remain delinquent taxes in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal accountant exert more effort to prepare the monthly trial balances and bank reconciliation statements. In his absence, he should delegate the preparation of the required financial reports to one his staffs so that these can be submitted on time to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction:
Due to delay in closing and delay in arrival of bank statement, the monthly trial balance was not submitted on time.

But the quarterly FS was submitted in the DILG full disclosure portal every quarter and posted in 3 conspicuous places.
observations.reason: Delays were still noted for some financial reports.

observations.recommendation:
Required the officials and employees of the municipality to refund the additional benefits received during the year in the total amount of ₱1,587,000.00.

The Local Chief Executive who is primarily responsible for the preparation and execution of the annual and supplemental budgets should see to it that all government expenditures should be properly and adequately covered by an appropriation ordinance to avoid criminal and administrative penalties as provided under RA 7160 and other laws.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing payroll deduction for the disallowance and hopefully this CY 2021, it will be fully paid.
observations.reason: Payroll deduction had been noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant obtain a formal estimate of recoverable service amount for the unserviceable items, compare with the carrying amount and make as basis in providing an allowance for impairment. Disclosures shall be provided in the financial statement in accordance with this standard. Management shall also consider disposal of the above items.
observations.managementAction:
No allowance for impairment loss was provided for those unserviceable PPE assets since carrying amount is equal to its salvage value. 

Disclosure was made in the 2020 Notes of Financial Statements regarding the unserviceable assets.
observations.reason: If the PPE items were already impaired, then the salvage values may not be realistic in determining its current recoverable amount.

observations.recommendation: Directed the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to locate and submit immediately the missing disbursement vouchers/checks, with all the supporting documents, so that these can be verified. Accordingly, all disbursement vouchers and the checks should be accounted for every month. Cash advance for salaries should also be submitted together with a copy of the payrolls, even though still without the signatures of the payees, to show that the amount of the cash advance is equal to the net payroll for a pay period, as required in Section 175 of the GAAM.
observations.managementAction:
The list of 112 DVs cannot be determined anymore due to non-turnover of records from previous accountant and the COA personnel cannot give us also the list of the lacking DVs. Furthermore, during the typhoon Yolanda, the accounting bodega was badly damaged, flood water and rainwater got inside and damaged the DVs from CY 2013 and below. Given the circumstances, it would be impracticable to recover such documents.

The management requested the COA auditor to hopefully close this finding due to impracticality and with consideration that in the succeeding years, all the DVs were completely submitted to COA for audit.
observations.reason: The Audit Team had noted that since the start of audit assignment at this LGU, all disbursement vouchers and supporting documents were completely submitted for audit.

observations.recommendation: Directed the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to locate and submit immediately the missing disbursement vouchers/checks, with all the supporting documents, so that these can be verified. CY 2011 | The list of 112 DVs cannot be determined anymore due to non-turnover of records plus the DVs from CY 2013 and below were destroyed by typhoon Yolanda since the bodega got damaged. It would be impracticable to recover such documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: direct the SWM focal
Person: to consider the appropriate means of communication depending on the
information that it wishes to pass on to the public and its stakeholders.
For instance, there should be public notices on television and on the radio
about who can access the landfill, the working hours at the landfill to
limit after-hours access and the schedule for collection of wastes from
households for the different zones. Websites have become increasingly
effective and economical in disseminating information. Therefore it is
important to have a specific link for Solid Waste Management within the
Municipality’s website so that it is easier for the public to have access to
information and policy on solid waste management as well as any issue
concerning solid waste management. To ensure effective implementation of the
solid waste management plan, a well-designed, current and targeted community
awareness program is required. The targeted audience should be considered
when determining how the information should be given to the public. The
urgency of the matter should also be considered so that everyone is aware of
the matter as soon as possible.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal  Mayor  to  require  the  Municipal
Accountant  to  book  up  all  public  infrastructures  that   were
transferred  to  the  Registry  of  Public  Infrastructure  to  the
Property, Plant and Equipment account for a  fair  presentation  of
the financial statements.

We recommended to the  Municipal  Mayor  to  require  the  Municipal
Accountant to make the necessary adjustment in the book of  accounts
and to reconcile with  the  Municipal  Treasurer  the  discrepancies
between the cash accounts  reported  in  the  SSAF/SRE  and  SFP/GL.
Thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments on the omissions/errors
to correct the reported balances of the  affected  accounts/balances
in the books and SSAF/SRE.

We recommended  to  the  Municipal  Mayor  to  direct  the  Municipal
Accountant  to  effect  the  necessary  adjustments  to  correct  the
omissions/errors in the books.

We recommended to the  Municipal  Mayor  to  require  the  Municipal
Accountant  to  properly  classify  the  Loans  Payable-Domestic  as
current  and  non-current  at  year-end   and   provide   sufficient
appropriate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to cause the provision  of  an
action plan or measures to catch up with  the  prescribed  financing
mix; thereafter, strictly conform with the mandated financing mix of
PRDP.

We further recommended that the Municipal  Treasurer  and  Municipal
Accountant should make sure that all disbursement for  PRDP  I-Build
subprojects should be reconciled with the  Certificate  of  Payments
approved by the DA.

We recommended to the  Municipal  Mayor  to  require  the  Municipal
Treasurer as the Petty Cash Custodian to:


a. use the Petty Cash Fund only for  non-recurring,  emergency  and
   petty expenses;


b. discontinue the practice of paying telephone and water bills out
   of the petty cash fund;


c. discontinue the practice of paying expenses for spare parts  for
   repair and maintenance of vehicles  and  equipment  out  of  the
   Petty Cash Fund. Instead, use check with  Disbursement  Vouchers
   supported with purchase request, quotations and other  necessary
   documents;


d. indicate in the Patty Cash Vouchers the complete information and
   specific details;


e. ensure that all charges to Petty Cash Fund  are  supported  with
   complete documentation as prescribed by  Section  1.2.2  of  COA
   Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to cause the implementation of
a  Payroll  System  to  distribute  salaries  via  Automated  Teller
Machines (ATMs).

We further  recommended  to  the  Municipal  Mayor  to  require  the
Municipal Treasurer to limit cash advances granted to the Disbursing
Officers within the limit of their maximum  cash  accountability  or
increase their fidelity bond sufficient to cover their maximum  cash
accountability.

We recommended to the  Municipal  Mayor  to  require  the  Municipal
Accountant:


a. to send within ten  (10)  days  before  the  expiration  of  the
   prescribed period a written  reminder  under  signature  of  the
   Mayor or his duly authorized representative enjoining  officials
   and employees concerned to settle their cash advance;


b. not to  sign  claims  for  additional  cash  advance  until  the
   previous cash advance is settled;


c. to cause the immediate withholding of  salaries  and  claims  of
   officers and employees who failed to settle their cash  advances
   within the prescribed period after being sent notice and demand;


d. to furnish all department heads  listings  of  all  unliquidated
   cash advances of employees under their charge to enhance efforts
   to effect liquidation.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the:

a.     Municipal     Budget     Officer     to      charge      routinary
   rehabilitation/repair/declogging of canal,  repair  and  maintenance
   of the street light or lightings of municipal hall, and  implementation
   of solid waste management programs to the  General  Fund  Proper
   based  on  a  valid  program  of  work  and  to   appropriate   the
   20%  DF  for  investment  or  capital  expenditures.

b. Municipal Engineer to prepare detailed  Program  of  Work  with  exact
   location and quantified  output  indicated  and  with  realistic  cost
   estimates that would maximize  the  funds  allocated  for  a  specific
   project, complete with the plans and drawings as warranted.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor:


a. to require the BAC to give utmost priority to the bidding process
   and to be on a “jury duty”  until  the  Notice  of  Award  is
   issued by the HOPE in order to complete the  entire  procurement
   process at the earliest possible time.


b. being the head of the Municipal Development Council,  to  ensure
   that only development programs and  projects  that  partake  the
   nature of investment and capital expenditure  are  included  the
   Annual Investment Plan (AIP) of the 20% DF as  mandated  by  the
   DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-01.


c. to direct the Municipal Budget Officer to  make  sure  that  all
   programs and activities  that  are  to  be  implemented  by  the
   Municipality under the 20% DF should contain details as  to  the
   amount, name, nature  and  place  to  be  undertaken.  Lump  sum
   appropriation for PPAs should be discouraged.

We recommended to the Municipal  Mayor  to  direct  the  LDRRMC  to
implement projects from the unexpended LDRRMF  in  accordance  with
the approved LDRRMFIP. Utilization of the unexpended LDRRMF must be
supported with a revised AIP duly approved by the Local  Sanggunian
and Municipal Mayor upon recommendation of the LDRRMC.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the  Disaster  Risk
Reduction Officer to:


a) coordinate with the Municipal  Budget  Officer  for  the  proper
   charging of expenditures in LDRRMF.


b) monitor the extent of the  implementation  of  the  LDRRMFIP  by
   coordinating  with  the  members  of  the  BAC  for  the  proper
   scheduling of procurements for PPAs included in the LDRRMFIP  to
   ensure that the public receive the benefits that may accrue from
   the utilization of the fund


c) coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in the  preparation  of
   the  Monthly Report on  Sources  and  Utilization  of  DRRMF  to
   ensure that  expenses  incurred  were  in  accordance  with  the
   approved LDRRMFIP


d) submit the required Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
   DRRMF to the Municipal Accountant so that the said report can be
   submitted to COA on or before the 15th day  after  end  of  each
   month pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:


a. direct the  Municipal  Accountant  and  Municipal  Treasurer  to
   disburse  funds  strictly  in  accordance  with  the  work   and
   financial plan timelines,  otherwise,  a  revised  and  approved
   disbursement schedule shall be submitted; and to fast track  the
   disbursement  process  while  ensuring  complete  and   adequate
   supporting documents to facilitate submission of liquidation  to
   the Audit Team.


b. direct  the  Municipal  Engineer  to  strictly  monitor  project
   implementation.

We recommended to the  Municipal  Mayor  to  direct  the  Municipal
Engineer:


a. to ensure that all conditions preliminary to the commencement of
   construction have been addressed and that a certification  shall
   be executed  that  detailed  preliminary  engineering  has  been
   conducted prior to the start of procurement activities to ensure
   that necessary requirements and documents have  been  undertaken
   for the project to be completed as planned;


b.  to  review  thoroughly  the  request  for  time  extension   by
   contractors based on complete and relevant documents  that  will
   enable the assessment  of  the  reasonableness  of  the  reasons
   cited;


c. to specify in the notice of suspension of work the period within
   which work is suspended and the reason for granting the  request
   to facilitate monitoring;


d. to ensure the timely issuance of notice of resumption  of  work,
   so work is started by  the  contractors  immediately  after  the
   period of work suspension; and


e. to maintain complete and orderly files of  documents  pertaining
   to  project  implementation  such  as  approved  contract   time
   extensions, notices of suspension of work and resumption of work
   to  facilitate  review  and  monitoring,  and  to  ensure   that
   appropriate action are acted on time.


We further recommended to the Municipal Mayor:


a. to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)  (a)  to  properly
   plan the procurement of infrastructure projects; (b) to initiate
   the forfeiture of Performance Security of erring contractors  in
   favor of the Municipality in case of non-completion of  projects
   within the contract period; and (c) to initiate the sanction  of
   blacklisting erring contractors, if warranted.


b. to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal  Treasurer
   to see to it that liquidated damages will be effected  on  every
   day of delay until the project is completed and accepted by  the
   officials concerned.

We recommended to the Municipal  Mayor  to  require  the  Municipal
Accountant to send  billings  to  the  contractor  for  the  unpaid
liquidated damages or may  collect  such  liquidated  damages  from
other securities posted by them, pursuant to regulations.

We further recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the members
of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to rescind  the  contract  and
to impose appropriate sanctions on  top  of  the  liquidated  damages
to be paid for contracts whose liquidated  damages  exceeded  the
10% ceiling pursuant to  the  RIRR  of  RA  No.  9184  to  address
the continuing delay in the completion of infrastructure projects.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor:


a. to direct Municipal officials concerned  to  limit  the  grant  of
   overtime  services  only   when   necessary   and   within   its
   appropriation.


b. to direct the department  heads  to  formulate  a  detailed  and
   systematic plan of  activities.  The  planned  activities  shall
   integrate therewith the volume  and  frequency  of  regular  and
   routine tasks to be performed to ensure that  overtime  services
   will be avoided;


c. to consider hiring additional competent and qualified  employees
   to understaffed departments to minimize the  need  for  overtime
   services without undermining  efficiency  and  effectiveness  of
   services;


d. to direct the Municipal Accountant to make  sure  that  overtime
   services have proper and prior authorization before  the conduct
   of  the  same  and  that  overtime  claims  are  supported  with
   quantified accomplishment report.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor:


a. to direct the Local Finance Committee to adhere to Paragraph (a)
   and (b) of Section 316 of the  Local  Government  Code  of  1991
   which requires that  budget  should  be  lodged  in  the  income
   reasonably projected and collectible for the ensuing fiscal year
   and that appropriately support the budget;


b. to direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit Status Report on the
   Revenue Code;


c. to coordinate with the members of the Sangguniang Bayan to  look
   into the possible updating of the  Revenue  Code  based  on  the
   report of the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended to the Municipal  Mayor  to  direct  the  GAD  Focal
Person to adhere to the provisions of  PCW-NEDA-DBM-DILG  JMC  Nos.
2013-001 and 2016-001 and to ensure that the PPAs included  in  the
GAD Plan and Budget address valid GAD issues identified through the
conduct of gender analysis.

We recommended to  the  Municipal  Mayor  to  direct  the  Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to ensure full settlement of  the
remaining disallowance of P994,000.00.

We recommended to the  Municipal  Mayor  to  direct  the  Municipal
Accountant to withhold the applicable tax rate from  (1)  honoraria
to resource  speakers  on  different  training  and  seminars,  (2)
honoraria of consultants of different projects, (3) honoraria of
personnel of national offices assigned  in  the  Municipality,  (4)
salaries and wages of contractual PRDP personnel, and (5) honoraria
of personnel of the Municipality designated as members of  BAC  and
oversight committee of the implementation of PRDP projects.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor and the local sanggunian to:


• craft ordinances and issue executive  orders  related  to  waste
  management and environmental protection;
• perfect the current waste management system of the Municipality,
  including, garbage classification, collection, and recycling;
• add more waste recycling facilities; and,
• focus on the recycling methods of the recyclables.

Directed the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to locate and submit immediately the missing disbursement vouchers/checks, with all the supporting documents, so that these can be verified. Accordingly, all disbursement vouchers and the checks should be accounted for every month. Cash advance for salaries should also be submitted together with a copy of the payrolls, even though still without the signatures of the payees, to show that the amount of the cash advance is equal to the net payroll for a pay period, as required in Section 175 of the GAAM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the officers concerned be required to submit a written and satisfactory justification for the option made, otherwise direct the persons who attended the seminars to refund the amounts in excess of the allowable travel expenses for a seminar in Cebu City. Henceforth, management should see to it that all resources of the government shall be managed, expended or utilized in accordance with law and regulations, and safeguarded against loss or wastage through illegal or improper disposition with a view to ensuing efficiency, economy and effectiveness in government operations.
observations.managementAction:
Since the previous municipal accountant retired mid 2015 and the new accountant assumed on the late 2015. The new accountant has no records as to these travel and seminars for it to be justified by the concerned personnel.

Prospectively, seminars with Cebu City leg will be utilized or availed with instead of outside Cebu to manage government resources well.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal accountant exert more effort to prepare the monthly trial balances and bank reconciliation statements. In his absence, he should delegate the preparation of the required financial reports to one his staffs so that these can be submitted on time to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Trial balance, Bank reconciliation and Quarterly FS were not submitted within the prescribed period due to delay in monthly closing, DVs were not returned to accounting on time.
observations.reason: Submitted but beyond the deadline set by COA.

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Accountant to comply with the deficiencies detailed in the attached schedule. Stop the practice of issuing and signing checks even without the supporting papers. Henceforth, all disbursement vouchers should bear complete documentation before these are submitted to the COA for verification.
observations.managementAction: If this pertains to the travel claims, there is a difficulty in obtaining supporting documents on bus and taxi fare due to passage of time. But starting 2015, claims on bus and taxi fare are based on the regular fare in the absence of actual receipt.
observations.reason: Non-submission of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit his collections intact daily in conformity with Section 89 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units Volume I to safeguard government funds from risk of loss.  Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer exert extra effort to reconcile monthly the balance of cash in vault account between the cashbook and general ledger to correct immediately the difference noted in order to establish reliable cash in vault balance.
observations.managementAction: In response to the above audit observation, the collections as of December 31, 2017 were not deposited intact due to some adjustments that were only noted during the preparation and reconciliation of abstract of collections. We need to wait for the reconciliation before we could close our cash book. Considering its December, volume of taxpayers will pay its property tax thus, the preparation and reconciliation of Abstract of Collections will take time. But rest assured the collections were deposited on the bank and record shows that on January 9 &17, 2018, the collections (cash in vault) had no balance already.
observations.reason: As of year-end 2018, undeposited collections amounted to ₱1,326,646.92. (Note that per validation, bulk of the undeposited collections pertains to checks received at year end which were already deposited on January 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconciles the balances of the reciprocal accounts under the three funds and book the necessary adjustments to bring their balances into agreement.  Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should closely monitor transactions affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure that these are recorded in the different funds simultaneously to avoid any error of omission.  Moreover, whenever possible, settle immediately transactions between funds to minimize efforts in monitoring and reconciliation and more importantly avoid erroneous account balances.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant was directed to reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts and make the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason:
As of 2018, Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds remain unreconciled with the following balances:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the bookkeeper stop using the estimated amounts in recording the consumption/use of the inventory items but require the use of RIS.  Likewise, direct the Supply Officer to summarize the RIS monthly and submit to the Accountant for recording in the books.  Also, require the conduct of physical count of all inventory items semi-annually for monitoring and control
observations.managementAction: Since the GSO has limited bodega space, the LGU practiced decentralized purchases wherein every requesting department process its own purchases on supplies and issued immediately to them. Thus, office supplies were expensed upon issuance.
observations.reason: No inventory count for supplies was conducted. Further, no consumption report was submitted.

observations.recommendation:
Required the officials and employees of the municipality to refund the additional benefits received during the year in the total amount of P1,587,000.00.

The Local Chief Executive who is primarily responsible for the preparation and execution of the annual and supplemental budgets should see to it that all government expenditures should be properly and adequately covered by an appropriation ordinance to avoid criminal and administrative penalties as provided under RA 7160 and other laws.
observations.managementAction: Management claimed that they did not exceed the 55% limitation however, they are awaiting the issuance of the notice of disallowance for the excess cash gift.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned BAC personnel submit justification on why the excess honoraria paid to them shall not be disallowed in audit. Prospectively, LGU shall observe the specific guidelines in the proper computation of honoraria for personnel involved in government procurement as provided in the Revised IRR of RA 9184 particularly in the observance of its maximum rates and limit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the guidelines under Section 6, Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184, and ensure that the required retention money are deducted from every progress payments until 50% completion of the infrastructure projects, or when work is not yet satisfactorily done and on schedule beyond this percentage of completion.

We recommended that Management conduct competitive bidding or any alternative mode when justified by conditions in the procurement of catering services for its various activities following the guidelines prescribed in GPPB Resolution No. 12-2017.

We recommended that the BAC submit a valid explanation in regards to the deficiencies observed. Prospectively, BAC should ensure that the Procuring Entity abides with the standards set forth in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to protect the interest of the Government and to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Accounting and Treasury Units implement appropriate controls so that the salary deductions allowed in the GAA will not result on the net take home pay to fall below the required threshold of P5,000. Personnel with loan amortizations which cannot be allowed anymore to be deducted from their salary and benefits may opt to pay directly to their respective lenders.

We recommended that the statutory positions for DRRM staff as required in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC joint memorandum circular, be filled up by qualified personnel instead of through job order contract, to ensure government accountability.

We recommended that BAC submit a valid explanation why no mandatory review and revision of specifications were done after failure of the first bidding of the foregoing procurements. Prospectively, BAC shall observe proper procurement planning by defining project requirements and bidding or procurement strategies in the PPMP so that the Procuring Entity can be able to achieve its procurement objectives.

1.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical
count of PPE and come up with the RPCPPE.
 direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to determine
the validity of the PPE account through reconciliation of the accounting
records and the actual inventory.
 require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the PPE Ledger Card
(PPELC) for each class of PPE listing therein the data as to its acquisition,
description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and
other information about the property, plant and equipment, based on the
source documents of the transactions and drop from the books of accounts
the unserviceable properties after the Waste Material Report and the
Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property are duly
approved and evaluated by the COA.

2.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant-
designate to revert back the outstanding checks ranging from two (2) years to
four (4) years in the books as debit to cash in bank and credit prior year’s
adjustment duly supported with necessary required documents.

2.8 Also, the Municipal Treasurer be made to send letters to the payees that they
have pending claims and whether they are still interested to claim. Thus, a
check replacement shall be issued upon request of the payee with the certified
photocopy of the paid DV to be attached thereon by the payee.

3.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant:
 to send within ten (10) days before the expiration of the prescribed period
a written reminder under signature of the Mayor or his duly authorized
representative enjoining officials and employees concerned to settle their
cash advance;
 not to sign claims for additional cash advance until the previous cash
advance is settled;
 to cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and
employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed
period after being sent notice and demand;
 to furnish all department heads listings of all unliquidated cash advances
of employees under their charge to enhance efforts to effect liquidation.

4.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to reconcile its records for loans payable with DBP and prepare the necessary
journal entries to adjust the balance loans payable account

5.7 We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
draw the following entries to correct erroneous recording of expenses directly
attributed to secure the road right of way of the Farm to Market Road projects:
DR: Road Networks (07 03 010) P646,700.00
CR: Prior Period Adjustment (01 01 020) P646,700.00

6.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 direct the Municipal Agriculture Officer together with the Inventory
Committee to conduct physical inventory of the breeding stock/animals.
 require the MAO to conduct thorough analysis/investigation on those
recipients who refunded/sold the animals assigned/dispersed to them.
 require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the work, Other animals and
Breeding Stocks Ledger Card

7.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget
Officer to ensure that only projects aligned with the purpose/objectives of the
20% Development Fund be funded from such appropriation. The Municipal
Accountant should likewise see to it that expenses charged against the 20%
DF is in accordance with prescribed guidelines.

8.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget
Officer and Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to include in the
Annual Investment Plan the programmed appropriations and obligations for
disaster risk reduction and management program, project & activities
embodied in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment
Plan (LDRRMFIP) to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-
MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust
Fund.

9.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 Direct the Local Risk Reduction Management Council to prepare its risk
reduction and management plan in accordance with the provision of
Section 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2013-01 dated March 25, 2013;
 Compel the Municipal Budget Officer to charge disbursement out of the
70% of LDRRMF only to those that specifically used to support the risk
reduction and management activities of the LGU; and
 Request the Sangguniang Bayan to issue a resolution to reprogram items
in the Annual Budget and to prepare a revised Annual Investment Plan.

11.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer,
Municipal Agriculture Officer (MAO) and members of the Bids and Awards
Committee to select the best course of action whether to rescind the contract,
takeover the project or award the same to other qualified contractors to
address the continuing delay in the project completion, pursuant to regulations
of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

12.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
collect from the contractor the unrecouped balance of the advance payment
for mobilization amounting to P416,147.65.

13.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 cause the refund of P804,000.00 honoraria for lack of legal basis;
 refrain from granting honoraria to officials and personnel of the
Municipality since they are already properly compensated in the form of
salaries;
 ensure that the provision of Section 54 of the General Provision of the
GAA of 2018, DBM Circular 2007-002 and COA Circular 2012-001 are
strictly observed.

14.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No.
2012-001 and to exercise due diligence in the processing of claims. We
further recommend that before effecting payment complete documentations
must be observed.

15.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
enforce collections of delinquent market stall/space rental in order to augment
funds to finance development projects of the Municipality.

17.6 We recommended that a mechanism to measure the volume of waste and type
of waste should be placed at the site, which will provide baseline data to assist
in developing effective waste minimization strategies and future plans for
waste management.

18.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Focal Person to
maintain disposal site records in the CMRF.

19.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) cause the workers at the
CMRF to wear the available protective gears; (b) direct the SWM Focal
Person to ensure that any person entering the MRF must wear the proper
safety clothing and that the operations at the landfill are effective and safe.

20.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the HRMO to adhere to
the provisions of Section 77 of RA No. 7160 which provides that the
Municipality may hire Job-order for local projects only, authorized by the
Sangguniang Bayan.

21.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
see to it that programs, projects and activities are aligned with GAD-related
undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services,
protection and other assistance that may be provided.

22.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to exert effort in enforcing settlement of audit disallowances.
Ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies
shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable in
accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September
15, 2009.

23.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
remit intact all taxes withheld. The Municipal Accountant should closely
monitor the withholding of taxes while the Municipal Treasurer should remit
promptly and intact the same to the BIR to avoid interests and penalties on
delayed remittances and accumulation of unremitted taxes in compliance to
BIR regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Municipal Physical Inventory Committee headed by the
Municipal Treasurer to:
a. conduct a complete physical count of Property Plant and
Equipment.
b. submit to the Auditor the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant
and Equipment (RCPPE) duly reconciled with the accounting records.
c. list all Property, Plant and Equipment found on hand, classifying them into
serviceable or unserviceable.
d. maintain updated property cards per category on all property plant and
equipment as recorded in the list of Property, Plant and Equipment of
accounting records.
e. reconcile property records ensuring that the balances shown in the prepared
RCPPE agree with the supply records and the book balances in the
accounting records, and vice versa.
f. renew and maintain all Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) for
the signature of the holder of each item listed in the prepared RCPPE.
2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the designated - Municipal Accountant to
determine the validity of the PPE account through reconciliation of the
accounting records and the actual inventory.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to send within ten (10) days before the
expiration of the prescribed period a written reminder under signature of the
LCE or his duly authorized representative enjoining officials and employees
concerned to settle their cash advance.
b. Cause the immediate withholding of salaries and claims of officers and
employees who failed to settle their cash advances within the prescribed
period pursuant to Section 5.1.3 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 after
being sent notice of demand. . The Municipal Accountant is authorized to
delete the name of the official and /or employee from the subsequent payroll
until that the cash advance has been fully liquidated pursuant to COA Circular
96-004.
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Budget
Officer to refrain from signing cash advance vouchers and obligation request
of officials and employees with outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Accountant to maintain special account for all loan proceeds from which financial
reports shall be prepared.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the officers and
employees to refund the amount collected in the absence of legal basis.
Management should desist and stop granting additional benefits unless in
pursuance of a lawful appropriation and/or other statutory authority.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) cause the submission of justification why the 20% development was
utilized for expenditures not in -consonance with DILG and DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2017 dated February 22, 2017;
b) Compel the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to
include in the LGUs Annual Investment Plan priority projects which are
capital in nature and those aligned with the development projects of the
20% Development Fund in order to attain environmental and socio-
economic development in the Municipality of Sogod.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Budget
Officer to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 15 of Republic Act 9344
known as Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006 and appropriate the required
allocation of 1% of its IRA for the LCPC activities. Furthermore, the LCE should
cause the creation of a Local Council for the protection of children which will
develop, identify and implement the programs, projects and activities using the said
fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to record in the books of accounts of the LGU the medical equipment
received from the Department of Health, Regional Office No. VIII to reflect the
accurate asset account in the financial statements. Also, we recommended that the
RHU Municipal Officer inform the Municipal Accountant on whatever equipment
received from the Department of Health for purposes of recording in the LGU’s
books of accounts and for monitoring of the assets.

We recommended that all Revenue Collectors be strictly required to submit
the monthly RAAF to be consolidated by the Municipal Treasurer thru CRAAF,

Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer shall be required to conduct regular physical count of properties
Moreover, specific courses of actions shall be carried out as follows, among others:  a)  in the absence of a GSO, a property custodian in the treasurer’s office to take custody of all properties and who will be responsible for the maintenance of PPE cards shall be designated;  b)  in the absence of a centralized stockroom for  supplies, a property custodian for every office who shall be designated to take custody of all inventories and  maintain stock cards;  c) a committee who will  conduct physical  count  regularly  of all inventories as well as PPE items should be created; and d) the Municipal Accountant shall maintain a complete and updated subsidiary ledgers for inventories and PPE items.  All records shall be updated and reconciled regularly with issuances of inventories and PPE items duly supported with Requisition Issue Slips for inventories and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE).

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the transfer of  property, plant equipment funded out from  Trust Fund and BUB funds to General Fund in order to reflect an accurate and reliable balances in the financial statements.  Furthermore, property, plant and equipment acquired or procured must be properly identified to facilitate physical inventory taking of properties.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee prepare a BAC Resolution justifying and recommending to the head of the procuring entity when an alternative mode of procurement is resorted in compliance with provisions of RA  9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, being the chair of the Local School Board observe the provisions of  RA 7160, DECS guidelines and other existing rules and regulations so that priority expenses and projects as mandated under SEF could be implemented.

observations.managementAction: CY 2010 The Municipal Mayor issued a memorandum for the payroll deduction. A Notice of Disallowance was issued and refunds were made through payroll deduction.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the officers concerned be required to submit a written and satisfactory justification for the option made, otherwise direct the persons who attended the seminars to refund the amounts in excess of the allowable travel expenses for a seminar in Cebu City. Henceforth, management should see to it that all resources of the government shall be managed, expended or utilized in accordance with law and regulations, and safeguarded against loss or wastage through illegal or improper disposition with a view to ensuing efficiency, economy and effectiveness in government operations.
observations.managementAction: CY 2010 The Municipal Mayor issued a memorandum to the OIC Municipal Accountant to look for vouchers related to the said travels.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal accountant exert more effort to prepare the monthly trial balances and bank reconciliation statements. In his absence, he should delegate the preparation of the required financial reports to one his staffs so that these can be submitted on time to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: CY 2009 A memorandum was issued directing the OIC-Municipal Accountant to exert effort to prepare and submit monthly the required reports.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the provisions of Sec. 5.1.3 of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 2007 be continually applied, that the local chief executive should order the withholding of the salaries and any money due to these officials and employees with unsettled cash advances and no cash advance shall be granted to them unless these overdue cash advances are settled or liquidated.
observations.managementAction: No more cash advances were granted to officials/ employees with outstanding cash advances. Cash advances for CY 2007, 2008 and 2009 have already been paid with outstanding cash through salary deduction except for those officials and employees who were deceased or separated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the agency head require the Property unit to reconcile the result of the physical count with the accounting records to arrive at the correct balance. Also, instruct the Accountant and Property Officer to observe the procedures on the preparation, proper recording and maintenance/updating of the SL for Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards in accordance with PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Management said that they created an PPE inventory team and maintain the PPE Records Ledger Cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer deposit his collections intact daily in conformity with Section 89 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units Volume I to safeguard government funds from risk of loss. Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer exert extra effort to reconcile monthly the balance of cash in vault account between the cashbook and general ledger to correct immediately the difference noted in order to establish reliable cash in vault balance.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 The Municipal Mayor issued a memorandum directing the OIC- Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their books and the Municipal Treasurer to deposit his collections daily.

observations.recommendation: Require the officials and employees of the municipality to refund the additional benefits received during the year in the total amount of P1,587,000.00. The Local Chief Executive who is primarily responsible for the preparation and execution of the annual and supplemental budgets should see to it that all government expenditures should be properly and adequately covered by an appropriation ordinance to avoid criminal and administrative penalties as provided under RA 7160 and other laws.
observations.managementAction: CY 2007 Management claimed that they did not exceed the 55% however, they are awaiting the issuance of the notice of disallowance for the excess cash gift.
